translations.View your time entries in a calendar for: View your time entries in a calendar format
translations.No time entries for this day: No time entries for this day
translations.This quick tour will help you get starte: This quick tour will help you get started with time tracking. You can skip this tour at any time.
translations.ArbeitszeitCheck helps you track your wo: ArbeitszeitCheck helps you track your working hours while ensuring compliance with German labor law (ArbZG) and GDPR requirements.
translations.Use the \"Clock In\" button to start tra: Use the "Clock In" button to start tracking your work time. When you finish, click "Clock Out" to stop.
translations.The system automatically tracks your wor: The system automatically tracks your working hours and ensures compliance with legal requirements.
translations.After 6 hours of work, you need at least: After 6 hours of work, you need at least 30 minutes of break time. After 9 hours, you need 45 minutes.
translations.Use \"Start Break\" and \"End Break\" to: Use "Start Break" and "End Break" to record your breaks. The system will remind you if you forget.
translations.The dashboard shows your daily, weekly,: The dashboard shows your daily, weekly, and monthly working hours, overtime balance, and vacation days remaining.
translations.You can also view your time entries in a: You can also view your time entries in a calendar or timeline format.
translations.You can request vacation days, sick leav: You can request vacation days, sick leave, or other absences through the Absences section.
translations.Your manager will review and approve or: Your manager will review and approve or reject your requests.
translations.Under GDPR, you have the right to access: Under GDPR, you have the right to access your personal data. You can export all your time tracking data at any time.
translations.Go to Settings to export your data in JS: Go to Settings to export your data in JSON format.
translations.You now know the basics of ArbeitszeitCh: You now know the basics of ArbeitszeitCheck. Start by clocking in to begin tracking your time!
translations.If you need help at any time, check the: If you need help at any time, check the documentation or contact your administrator.
translations.View and manage your time entries: View and manage your time entries
translations.View your time entries in calendar forma: View your time entries in calendar format
translations.View your time entries in timeline forma: View your time entries in timeline format
translations.All working time regulations are being f: All working time regulations are being followed correctly

translations.Start tracking your time by clicking the: Start tracking your time by clicking the Clock In button above
translations.Overview of your working time and status: Overview of your working time and status
translations.Clock in to start tracking time: Clock in to start tracking time
translations.Clock out to end tracking time: Clock out to end tracking time
translations.You have worked {hours} hours today. Acc: You have worked {hours} hours today. According to German labor law, you must take a {minutes}-minute break. You still need {remaining} minutes of break time.
translations.Use these buttons to track your working: Use these buttons to track your working hours. Click "Clock In" when you start working and "Clock Out" when you finish.
translations.Click this button when you start working: Click this button when you start working. The system will track your working hours automatically.
translations.Click this button when you finish workin: Click this button when you finish working. The system will stop tracking your working hours.
translations.Click to start your break. German labor: Click to start your break. German labor law requires breaks after 6 hours of work.
translations.Click to end your break and resume worki: Click to end your break and resume working.
translations.Overview of your working hours, break ti: Overview of your working hours, break time, and overtime for today.
translations.Compliance status shows whether your wor: Compliance status shows whether your working hours follow German labor law (ArbZG) requirements. Click for more information.
translations.Maximum 8 hours of work per day (can be: Maximum 8 hours of work per day (can be extended to 10 hours in special cases)
translations.At least 30 minutes break after 6 hours: At least 30 minutes break after 6 hours of work
translations.At least 45 minutes break after 9 hours: At least 45 minutes break after 9 hours of work
translations.At least 11 hours of rest between shifts: At least 11 hours of rest between shifts
translations.Your current working status and today's: Your current working status and today's working hours.
Your most recent time tracking entries. Click on any entry to view or edit details.
translations.You can provide an optional reason for r: You can provide an optional reason for rejecting this request. This will be visible to the employee.

translations.Select the type of report you want to ge: Select the type of report you want to generate. Each report type provides different insights into working hours, overtime, and absences.
translations.Select the date for the daily report: Select the date for the daily report
translations.Select the week start date (Monday) to g: Select the week start date (Monday) to generate a weekly report with daily breakdown.
translations.Select a month to generate a monthly rep: Select a month to generate a monthly report with user breakdown and statistics.
translations.Select a date range to analyze overtime: Select a date range to analyze overtime and undertime across all users.
translations.Select a date range to generate a report: Select a date range to generate a report on absences by type, status, and user.
translations.Select a date range and team member to g: Select a date range and team member to generate a comprehensive team report.
translations.Select the start date for the report per: Select the start date for the report period
translations.Select the end date for the report perio: Select the end date for the report period
translations.Select a team member to generate the tea: Select a team member to generate the team report. Multiple users can be added in future updates.
translations.Optionally select a specific user. Leave: Optionally select a specific user. Leave empty to include all users.
translations.Team reports require selecting a team me: Team reports require selecting a team member
translations.Your absence history will appear here: Your absence history will appear here
translations.Note: Team reports currently support sin: Note: Team reports currently support single user selection. Multiple users can be added in future updates.
translations.Monitor labor law compliance and violati: Monitor labor law compliance and violations
translations.Enter your working times including break: Enter your working times including breaks. The system will automatically check compliance with German labor law (ArbZG).
translations.Add all breaks you took during this work: Add all breaks you took during this working day. German labor law requires at least 30 minutes break after 6 hours, and 45 minutes after 9 hours of work.
translations.End time must be after start time: End time must be after start time
translations.Break end time must be after break start: Break end time must be after break start time
translations.Break times must be within your working: Break times must be within your working hours
translations.German labor law (ArbZG) allows a maximu: German labor law (ArbZG) allows a maximum of 10 hours per day. You have entered {hours} hours.
translations.You have worked {hours} hours. The norma: You have worked {hours} hours. The normal maximum is 8 hours per day. Extended hours must be compensated within 6 months.
translations.German labor law requires at least 30 mi: German labor law requires at least 30 minutes break after 6 hours of work. You have only {minutes} minutes.
translations.You have worked {hours} hours. Make sure: You have worked {hours} hours. Make sure you have taken at least 30 minutes of break.
translations.German labor law requires at least 45 mi: German labor law requires at least 45 minutes break after 9 hours of work. You have only {minutes} minutes.
translations.Date, start time, and end time are requi: Date, start time, and end time are required

translations.Edit the working time model settings bel: Edit the working time model settings below. Changes will affect all users assigned to this model.

translations.Additional notes for the corrected entry: Additional notes for the corrected entry
translations.Working hours exceed the legal maximum o: Working hours exceed the legal maximum of 10 hours per day. This requires special approval.
translations.Working hours exceed the normal 8 hours: Working hours exceed the normal 8 hours per day. This requires special approval.
translations.After 9 hours of work, at least 45 minut: After 9 hours of work, at least 45 minutes of break time is required by German labor law (ArbZG).
translations.After 6 hours of work, at least 30 minut: After 6 hours of work, at least 30 minutes of break time is required by German labor law (ArbZG).

translations.Your working time model, vacation days,: Your working time model, vacation days, and working hours are assigned by your administrator. Contact your administrator if you have questions or need changes.
translations.This app helps you record your work time: This app helps you record your work time and follow German labor law. Here's how to get started:
translations.Click the \"Clock In\" button below when: Click the "Clock In" button below when you start work
translations.Click \"Clock Out\" when you finish work: Click "Clock Out" when you finish work
translations.The system will automatically track your: The system will automatically track your hours and remind you to take breaks
translations.You can also add time entries manually o: You can also add time entries manually or request vacation days in the "Absences" section
translations.Go to time entries to see how to add ent: Go to time entries to see how to add entries manually
translations.Click to hide this welcome message: Click to hide this welcome message
translations.See your current work status, today's ho: See your current work status, today's hours, and recent time entries
translations.Go to dashboard to see your work status: Go to dashboard to see your work status and today's hours
translations.Go to time entries to see all your worki: Go to time entries to see all your working time records
translations.Go to absences to request vacation or si: Go to absences to request vacation or sick leave
translations.Go to reports to create and download wor: Go to reports to create and download working time reports
translations.Go to settings to change your preference: Go to settings to change your preferences
translations.Track your working time and follow Germa: Track your working time and follow German labor law
translations.Dashboard: See your current work status,: Dashboard: See your current work status, today's hours, and recent time entries
translations.Time Entries: View, add, edit, or delete: Time Entries: View, add, edit, or delete all your working time records
translations.Absences: Request and manage vacation da: Absences: Request and manage vacation days, sick leave, and other time off
translations.Reports: Create and download reports abo: Reports: Create and download reports about your working time
translations.Calendar: View your working time and abs: Calendar: View your working time and absences in a calendar view
translations.Timeline: See your working time history: Timeline: See your working time history in chronological order
translations.Settings: Change your personal preferenc: Settings: Change your personal preferences and working time settings
translations.Go to calendar to see your working time: Go to calendar to see your working time in a calendar
translations.Go to timeline to see your working time: Go to timeline to see your working time history
translations.Set up how time tracking works for every: Set up how time tracking works for everyone in your organization
translations.An unexpected error occurred. Please try: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again. If the problem continues, contact your administrator.
translations.The system will automatically check if w: The system will automatically check if working hours follow German labor law. For example, it will warn if someone works more than 8 hours per day without a break.
translations.Require a reason when taking breaks: Require a reason when taking breaks
translations.When employees take breaks, they must wr: When employees take breaks, they must write a short reason. This helps ensure breaks are used correctly and comply with labor law.
translations.Send alerts when working time rules are: Send alerts when working time rules are broken
translations.When someone works too many hours or doe: When someone works too many hours or doesn't take required breaks, the system will send a notification to managers and the employee.
translations.The most hours someone can work in one d: The most hours someone can work in one day. German law allows up to 8 hours normally, or 10 hours in special cases. Example: Enter 10 to allow up to 10 hours per day.
translations.Minimum Rest Time Between Work Days: Minimum Rest Time Between Work Days
translations.How many hours of rest someone must have: How many hours of rest someone must have between the end of one work day and the start of the next. German law requires at least 11 hours. Example: If someone finishes work at 6 PM, they cannot start again before 5 AM the next day.
translations.The normal number of hours people work e: The normal number of hours people work each day. This is used for new employees until you set their individual working hours. Example: Enter 8 for a standard 8-hour workday.
translations.Select the German state where your organ: Select the German state where your organization is located. This ensures the system follows the correct labor laws for that state.
translations.How Long to Keep Time Records: How Long to Keep Time Records
translations.How many years to keep time tracking rec: How many years to keep time tracking records before they are automatically deleted. German law typically requires keeping records for 2 years. Example: Enter 2 to keep records for 2 years.
translations.Click to save all settings. Changes will: Click to save all settings. Changes will apply to all users.
translations.Cancel and go back to dashboard: Cancel and go back to dashboard
translations.Click to cancel and go back without savi: Click to cancel and go back without saving changes
translations.See how many employees are using the sys: See how many employees are using the system and check for any problems with working time rules
translations.Total number of employees who can use ti: Total number of employees who can use time tracking
translations.Number of employees who clocked in today: Number of employees who clocked in today
translations.Number of working time rule violations t: Number of working time rule violations that need attention
translations.Working time rule violations that need y: Working time rule violations that need your attention
translations.Great! All employees are following the w: Great! All employees are following the working time rules correctly.
translations.See how your team is doing with time tra: See how your team is doing with time tracking and check for any problems
translations.Check if your working time follows Germa: Check if your working time follows German labor law and see any problems that need fixing
translations.Your working time follows all German lab: Your working time follows all German labor law rules. Keep up the good work!
translations.There are some issues with your working: There are some issues with your working time that need attention. Please check the list below and fix them.
translations.How well you follow the rules:: How well you follow the rules:
translations.This shows how well your working time fo: This shows how well your working time follows German labor law. 100% means everything is perfect.
translations.See statistics about working time proble: See statistics about working time problems and how well employees follow the rules
translations.Total number of times working time rules: Total number of times working time rules were broken
translations.Number of problems that still need to be: Number of problems that still need to be fixed
translations.See what kinds of working time problems: See what kinds of working time problems occurred
translations.See how serious the problems were: See how serious the problems were
translations.List of times when working time rules we: List of times when working time rules were not followed correctly
translations.See when working time rules were not fol: See when working time rules were not followed and what needs to be fixed
translations.Filter by how serious the problem is: Filter by how serious the problem is
translations.Choose to see only serious problems, min: Choose to see only serious problems, minor problems, or all problems
translations.Click to show only the problems that mat: Click to show only the problems that match your filter settings
translations.Great! No working time rule violations w: Great! No working time rule violations were found for the selected time period.
translations.See a record of all actions taken in the: See a record of all actions taken in the time tracking system

translations.No activity was recorded for the selecte: No activity was recorded for the selected time period.
translations.Add employees to time tracking and set t: Add employees to time tracking and set their work schedules
translations.Search employees by name or email addres: Search employees by name or email address
translations.Type a name or email address to find emp: Type a name or email address to find employees quickly
translations.Click to reload the list and see any new: Click to reload the list and see any new employees
translations.Click to change this employee's work sch: Click to change this employee's work schedule or other settings
translations.Set up different work schedules and assi: Set up different work schedules and assign them to employees
translations.Click to create a new work schedule. For: Click to create a new work schedule. For example, you could create a model for full-time employees (8 hours per day) or part-time employees (4 hours per day).
translations.Click to change the working hours or nam: Click to change the working hours or name of this work schedule
translations.Click to delete this work schedule. This: Click to delete this work schedule. This cannot be undone, so make sure no employees are using it first.
translations.Are you sure you want to delete this wor:
Are you sure you want to delete this work schedule?

This will permanently remove this schedule. If any employees are using this schedule, you should assign them to a different schedule first.

This action cannot be undone.
translations.Request time off for vacation or sick le: Request time off for vacation or sick leave
translations.Click to request time off. You can reque: Click to request time off. You can request vacation days, sick leave, or other types of absences.
translations.Delete this time entry permanently. This: Delete this time entry permanently. This cannot be undone.
translations.Are you sure you want to delete this tim:
Are you sure you want to delete this time entry?

This will permanently remove this record of your working time. This action cannot be undone.
translations.View working hours for a specific day: View working hours for a specific day
translations.What information do you want in the repo: What information do you want in the report?
The first day to include in the report. Click the calendar icon to pick a date. Example: If you want a report for January, select January 1st.
translations.The last day to include in the report. C: The last day to include in the report. Click the calendar icon to pick a date. Example: If you want a report for January, select January 31st.
Choose how you want to save the report. PDF is best for printing or viewing. Excel or CSV is best if you want to edit the data in a spreadsheet program.
translations.Click to see what the report will look l: Click to see what the report will look like before downloading it
translations.Click to create the report and download: Click to create the report and download it to your computer
translations.Click to cancel and go back without gene: Click to cancel and go back without generating a report
translations.How many vacation days you get each year: How many vacation days you get each year. In Germany, the standard is 25 days. Example: Enter 25 if you get 25 vacation days per year.
translations.The system will automatically calculate: The system will automatically calculate when you need to take breaks according to German labor law. For example, if you work more than 6 hours, you must take at least a 30-minute break.
translations.Click to save your working time preferen: Click to save your working time preferences
translations.Your recent time entries will appear her: Your recent time entries will appear here. Start by clocking in to track your working time.
translations.fieldRequired: This field is required. Please fill in this field.
translations.emailInvalid: Please enter a valid email address. Example: name@example.com
translations.minLength: Please enter at least {min} characters. You entered {current} characters.
translations.maxLength: Please enter no more than {max} characters. You entered {current} characters.
translations.invalidFormat: The format is incorrect. Please check the format and try again.
translations.numberInvalid: Please enter a valid number. Example: {example}
translations.numberMin: Please enter a number that is at least {min}. You entered {current}.
translations.numberMax: Please enter a number that is no more than {max}. You entered {current}.
translations.timeInvalid: Please enter the time in this format: HH:MM (hours:minutes). Example: 09:00 for 9 in the morning, or 14:30 for 2:30 in the afternoon.
translations.dateInvalid: Please enter a valid date. Click the calendar icon to pick a date, or enter it in this format: YYYY-MM-DD. Example: 2024-01-15 for January 15, 2024.
translations.unexpectedError: Something went wrong. Please check your internet connection and try again. If the problem continues, contact support.

translations.Break must be at least 15 minutes long t: Break must be at least 15 minutes long to count toward legal break requirement (ArbZG §4). Current duration: %d minutes
translations.Warning: 6-month average working hours (: Warning: 6-month average working hours (%.2f h/day) exceeds 8 hours/day. 10-hour days are only allowed if the average does not exceed 8 hours (ArbZG §3).
translations.Warning: 6-month average weekly working: Warning: 6-month average weekly working hours (%.2f h/week) exceeds 48 hours/week (ArbZG §3).
translations.Record when you worked by entering the s: Record when you worked by entering the start and end times, and any breaks you took.

Edit your time entry. You can edit manual entries, entries with pending approval, or completed automatic entries from the last 2 weeks.

translations.German labor law compliant time tracking: German labor law compliant time tracking for Nextcloud
translations.Get a notification when it's time to tak: Get a notification when it's time to take a required break. For example, if you work more than 6 hours, you'll get a reminder to take at least a 30-minute break.
translations.Optional: Record your break times. Break: Optional: Record your break times. Breaks of at least 15 minutes count toward legal requirements (ArbZG §4).
translations.Select the day you worked (format: dd.mm: Select the day you worked (format: dd.mm.yyyy)
translations.Working hours automatically limited to %: Working hours automatically limited to %1$s hours (ArbZG §3). End time adjusted from %2$s to %3$s.
translations.Working hours (excluding breaks) exceed: Working hours (excluding breaks) exceed the legal maximum of
translations.Note: You are approaching the maximum wo: Note: You are approaching the maximum working hours. Extended hours must be compensated within 6 months (ArbZG §3).
translations.Break must be at least 15 minutes long t: Break must be at least 15 minutes long to count toward legal break requirement (ArbZG §4). Current duration:
